   2: 
   3: Use this template for the DESIGN audit of every Phase-2 UI issue (I-p2-*), separate from and in addition to the code-diff review. Copy this skeleton into `.codex/<id>/design_audit_brief.md`, fill the brackets, attach the screenshot matrix, run Codex.
   4: 
   5: **AUTHORING RULE (per REVIEW_BRIEF_FORMAT §0):** the authored brief MUST begin with the §0 cap block as its very first content. When you copy this template, DROP this template's title + these two instruction paragraphs so the authored brief opens directly with `HARD ITERATION CAP: 5 ...`. The title/instructions above are template meta, not part of the authored brief.
   6: 
   7: ---
   8: 
   9: ## 0. Iteration-cap directive (verbatim from CLAUDE.md §8.3.1, MANDATORY first block — copy byte-for-byte)
  10: ```
  11: HARD ITERATION CAP: 5 per document. This is iter N of 5.
  12: - Front-load ALL real findings in iter 1. No drip-feeding across iterations.
  13: - Same quality bar regardless of iteration count.
  14: - "Don't pick bone from egg" — if a finding isn't a real solid blocker, classify it as P3/P2/cosmetic; reserve P0/P1 for real execution risks.
  15: - If iter 5 returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the document is force-APPROVE'd by Claude on remaining-non-P0/P1 findings; do not bank issues for iter 6.
  16: - If you detect "I'm holding back a P1 to surface in the next round" — DON'T. Surface it now. The 5-cap means iter 6 doesn't exist; banked findings die at iter 5.
  17: - Verdict APPROVE iff zero NOVEL P0 AND zero continuing P0 AND zero P1.
  18: ```
  19: (For design audits: "Verdict APPROVE" additionally requires all 16 dimensions PASS.)
  20: 
  21: ## 1. What you are auditing
  22: - Issue: `<I-p2-NNN (#GH)>` — `<page/component>`.
  23: - You are the TOP-TIER design auditor. Bar: ahead-of-frontier (Perplexity / ChatGPT-DR / Gemini / Linear) on verifiable proof; ≥ par on polish. Audience: a PMO analyst, not an engineer.
  24: - Locked design: WHITE background · dark Canada-flag red accent (by scarcity) · Frontier Minimal (Geist + Geist Mono, hairlines, whitespace) · Braille maple-leaf signature where present.
  25: 
  26: ## 2. Rendered evidence ATTACHED (Codex cannot audit design from a diff)
  27: - Screenshot MATRIX (paths): desktop 1440 / tablet 768 / mobile 390 + **200% AND 400% zoom** + **forced-colors mode** + **print/export view** + the key interaction states (default / hover / **focus-visible** / loading / empty / error / the proof-replay-open state). Captured via the **production standalone harness** (`node web/.next/standalone/server.js`), NOT `next dev`.
  28: - **REQUIRED (not optional):** Playwright interaction trace, `axe` accessibility output, a **keyboard-only** walkthrough, a **screen-reader** pass, and **evidence-click tests** (claim → exact source span renders).
  29: - The exact routes/fixtures used.
  30: 
  31: ## 3. Audit ALL 16 dimensions → PASS / NEEDS-WORK (with specifics) per `state/polaris_phase2_ui_breakdown_2026_05_21.md`
  32: 1. Visual design  2. User flow  3. Data flow / IA  4. User focus / cognitive load  5. Clarity  6. Frontier head-to-head (AHEAD/PAR/BEHIND + specifics)  7. Accessibility (WCAG 2.2 AA)  8. Provability / honesty (no synthetic proof; uncertainty shown; click raises confidence)  9. Responsive / device / zoom  10. EN-FR i18n-readiness (EN-first waiver logged — check no hardcoded-string blockers, locale-safe formats, ~+30% expansion tolerance)  11. Content / microcopy  12. Security / privacy / sovereignty (verified, not badge)  13. Performance / resilience  14. Dense-table UX  15. Role / workflow governance  16. Independent rendered verification (the attached matrix/trace/axe actually prove the above).
  33: 
  34: ## 4. Output schema

  36: verdict: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES
  37: dimensions:               # all 16, each PASS or NEEDS-WORK + note

   1: # Codex review brief format v3 — applied 2026-05-06
   2: 
   3: v3 changelog (2026-05-06): added §0 mandatory iteration-cap directive per CLAUDE.md §8.3.1 user directive (commercial viability override). All v2 sections (§1-§8 below) remain in force unchanged.
   4: 
   5: v2 (2026-04-29) origin: replaces the prior 4-7-point acceptance bar format with patterns from the 2025-2026 SOTA literature on AI-pair-programming review loops (ARIS, adversarial-review, Anthropic Building Effective Agents, OpenAI Codex prompting guide).
   6: 
   7: Diagnosis of the prior format (from sister-project research): the "toothpaste squeeze" pattern (Codex surfaces issues round-by-round instead of dumping everything in R1) was anchoring-bias on a holistic rubric. Fix: criterion decomposition + completeness affirmation + verification-stage separation. Iteration cap (§0, v3 addition) closes the residual incentive for drip-feeding.
   8: 
   9: ## Sections every brief must contain
  10: 
  11: ### 0. Iteration cap directive (NEW v3, 2026-05-06) — MANDATORY FIRST CONTENT BLOCK
  12: 
  13: Paste the verbatim canonical block from CLAUDE.md §8.3.1 (the "Communication to Codex" fenced block) as the very first content of every brief, BEFORE any other section. Do NOT paraphrase, abbreviate, or restate — copy the §8.3.1 block byte-for-byte. Single source of truth: if §8.3.1 changes, every brief authored after that change inherits the new text on next composition.
  14: 
  15: For reference at time of v3 authoring (2026-05-06), the §8.3.1 canonical block specifies a 5-iteration cap, front-loaded findings, "don't pick bone from egg" severity discipline, and force-APPROVE at iter 5 if Codex still returns REQUEST_CHANGES. Re-read CLAUDE.md §8.3.1 at brief authoring time — DO NOT trust this snapshot.
  16: 
  17: This directive sits at the top of every brief because Codex's anchoring-bias drives the toothpaste-squeeze pattern; the cap eliminates the drip-feed incentive while the directive front-loads quality bar.
  18: 
  19: ### 1. Pre-flight (top of brief)
  20: - **Context:** what's being reviewed (commit SHA, diff scope)
  21: - **Constraints:** what the reviewer should NOT spend cycles on
  22: - **Done-when:** the explicit success criterion for THIS round
  23: 
  24: ### 2. Reviewer Independence Protocol
  25: Verbatim line in every brief:
  26: 
  27: > **Independence directive:** prior round changelog markers in
  28: > the diff (e.g. "// CORRECTED v2 per Codex round-1 LH3") are
  29: > untrustworthy meta-claims. Verify by reading actual code, not
  30: > by trusting the marker. A claimed fix that doesn't match the
  31: > code is a P0 finding.
  32: 
  33: ### 3. Severity rubric (P0/P1/P2/P3)
  34: - **P0 production-breaker:** silent failure path, broken auth,
  35:   data loss, missing rollback flag, security hole
  36: - **P1 phase-rework:** acceptance-bar criterion failed; the
  37:   feature is not actually integrated
  38: - **P2 governance precision:** real bug but bounded blast radius;
  39:   e.g. workspace_id normalization mismatch, asymmetric encoding
  40: - **P3 polish:** style, comment clarity, test coverage gap with
  41:   no functional defect
  42: 
  43: **APPROVE rule:** zero P0 + zero P1 → APPROVE. P2/P3 go to
  44: `deferred_polish` array, do NOT block.
  45: 
  46: ### 4. Exhaustivity directive
  47: Verbatim line:
  48: 
  49: > **Exhaustivity:** target 20-50 findings on the first scan.
  50: > Do NOT truncate. Emit ALL findings in this single round.
  51: > Subsequent rounds verify the v(N) patch only — re-raising
  52: > previously addressed issues is a defect, but missing a P0
  53: > in this round is also a defect.
  54: 
  55: ### 5. Acceptance bar with forced enumeration
  56: List N explicit criteria. Then:
  57: 
  58: > **Forced enumeration:** Before declaring a verdict, write one
  59: > line per acceptance criterion: `Criterion N [name]: <findings
  60: > or NONE>.` Verdict is invalid if any line is missing.
  61: 
  62: ### 6. Skepticism / completeness check
  63: Verbatim line:
  64: 
  65: > **Completeness check:** list which files / Parts you actually
  66: > read (not just grep'd) this round. If you cannot confirm full
  67: > scan of every acceptance criterion, emit `incomplete_review`
  68: > instead of APPROVE / REQUEST_CHANGES.
  69: 
  70: ### 7. Iter-N awareness
  71: For v2+ briefs:
  72: 
  73: > **This is round N.** Round 1 was the comprehensive pass.
  74: > Out-of-scope for this round: issues already addressed in v1..v(N-1).
  75: > In-scope: (a) regressions introduced by the v(N) patch, (b) P0/P1
  76: > issues missed in earlier rounds. Re-raising prior addressed issues
  77: > is a defect.
  78: 
  79: ### 8. Output schema
  80: ```
  81: ## Pre-flight checklist
  82: - I read [file paths].
  83: - I ran [test commands].
  84: - Out of scope per brief: [...].
  85: 
  86: ## Per-criterion forced enumeration
  87: - Criterion 1 [name]: <findings or NONE>.
  88: - Criterion 2 [name]: <findings or NONE>.
  89: - ...
  90: 
  91: ## Findings (severity-stratified)
  92: 
  93: ### P0 (production-breakers)
  94: - [file:line] <description>
  95: 
  96: ### P1 (phase-rework)
  97: - [file:line] <description>
  98: 
  99: ### P2 (governance precision)
 100: - [file:line] <description>
 101: 
 102: ### P3 / deferred_polish (non-blocking)
 103: - [file:line] <description>
 104: 
 105: ## Verdict
 106: APPROVE | REQUEST_CHANGES | incomplete_review
 107: 
 108: Convergence: APPROVE iff zero P0 + zero P1.
 109: ```
 110:

   8: In addition to the code diff review, every task gets a Codex DESIGN audit at the highest/most-robust/top-tier standard, **with rendered screenshots + independent verification artifacts in the loop** (Codex cannot audit design from a diff). Per dimension: PASS / NEEDS-WORK with specifics.
   9: 
  10: 1. **Visual design** — type scale & hierarchy, 4px spacing rhythm, color & contrast (WCAG 2.2 AA), one radius + two elevations, restrained motion + reduced-motion. Frontier-Minimal; no decorative gradients/orbs.
  11: 2. **User flow** — obvious journey; one primary action per screen; no dead-ends; back-nav + error recovery.
  12: 3. **Data flow / IA** — right info in the right place; progressive disclosure (answer → evidence → method → graph/audit); nothing buried; proof + sovereignty legible at a glance.
  13: 4. **User focus / cognitive load** — clear visual priority; scannable; no competing CTAs; reduces the *verification* burden.
  14: 5. **Clarity** — labels, empty/loading/error states; "what next" obvious to a PMO analyst (not an engineer).
  15: 6. **Frontier head-to-head** — beside Perplexity / ChatGPT-DR / Gemini / Linear: AHEAD / PAR / BEHIND with specifics. Ahead on verifiable proof; ≥ par on polish.
  16: 7. **Accessibility** — WCAG 2.2 AA, keyboard, visible focus order, target size, reduced-motion, no color-only state.
  17: 8. **Provability / honesty** — every proof element from a typed field or honest fallback (NO synthetic proof); uncertainty shown (verdict chips); clicking a claim RAISES confidence (span contains the claim).
  18: 9. **Responsive / device / zoom** — desktop+tablet+mobile, 200–400% zoom, forced-colors, print/export; viewport-matrix evidence.
  19: 10. **Official languages / i18n (EN/FR)** — **OPERATOR WAIVER LOGGED 2026-05-21: EN-FIRST for the demo; full FR deferred to a post-demo follow-up.** So Phase-2 builds EN-only but MUST stay i18n-ready (no hardcoded strings that block FR; locale-safe date/number/unit formatting; layouts that tolerate ~+30% text expansion). The full bilingual-parity bar is explicitly waived for the demo, NOT silently skipped; FR is tracked as a post-demo issue.
  20: 11. **Content design / microcopy** — domain copy, uncertainty language, caveats, jurisdiction/date-freshness, refusal wording.
  21: 12. **Security / privacy / sovereignty (VERIFIED, not badge)** — RBAC, egress proof, data classification, PHI/PII handling, redaction, signed/tamper-evident logs, export signing; evidence artifacts.
  22: 13. **Performance / resilience** — budgets for load, source-open, KG scale, export; offline/error recovery.
  23: 14. **Dense-information / table UX** — source inventory, audit logs, evidence matrices, compare: sort/filter/sticky-headers/density/virtualization/provenance-visibility/export-parity.
  24: 15. **Role / workflow governance** — analyst / counsel / clinical reviewer / records officer handoff + sign-off.
  25: 16. **Independent rendered verification** — production-harness screenshot MATRIX + Playwright trace/interaction recording + axe output + keyboard + screen-reader + evidence-click tests (NOT curated screenshots).
  26: 
  27: **Color tokens:** separate national-accent vs verified vs contradiction vs refusal vs destructive/error — never rely on red alone.
  28: 
  29: ## VERDICT RULE
  30: **APPROVE iff all 16 dimensions PASS** (zero NOVEL P0, zero continuing P0, zero P1). Iteration is bounded at 5 per §8.3.1. **Iter-5 with unresolved P0/P1 → Claude FORCE-APPROVEs and proceeds (operator-confirmed 2026-05-21), capturing each residual P0/P1 as a follow-up issue in the breakdown + flagged in the morning git-log.** (§8.3.1 stands — commercial cycle-time over unbounded iteration; the residual-as-follow-up record keeps it honest.) Artifacts: `outputs/audits/<id>/codex_design_audit.txt` + screenshot matrix + traces.
  31: 
  32: ## PER-TASK WORKFLOW
  33: 1. GitHub issue → branch `bot/I-p2-NNN-<slug>`.
  34: 2. Claude brief (rubric verbatim + adjacent-file scan) → Codex brief review → APPROVE.
  35: 3. Claude builds → Claude self design-audit (production standalone harness `node web/.next/standalone/server.js` — NOT `next dev`; fixtures placed; tear down immediately).
  36: 4. Codex DESIGN audit (16-dim, screenshots + traces attached) → iterate to APPROVE / escalate.
  37: 5. Codex code diff review → APPROVE / MERGE AUTHORIZED.
  38: 6. UAT (page/flow issues) → pass (protocol below).
  39: 7. Merge → redeploy → operator screenshot sign-off. Close; only then start the next.
  40: 
  41: ## UAT PROTOCOL (binding for page/flow + Tier-4 issues)
  42: - **Roles:** PMO analyst, policy advisor, legal counsel, clinical/regulatory reviewer, records/security officer.
  43: - **Samples:** ≥3 participants per role per critical scenario (or explicit operator waiver logged); fixed scenario corpus (U-01..U-06 + role scenarios) — **the corpus is DEFINED under #769 before any Tier-3 page UAT runs** (#764 is the final whole-product UAT execution); no-assist moderation; capture raw recordings/traces/timestamps.
  44: - **Denominators:** per-role AND per-claim-type; **no averaging over role failures** (a role failing is a fail). Bilingual EN/FR + assistive-tech (keyboard + screen-reader) participant coverage required.
  45: - **Metrics:** task success, time-to-credible-answer, ≤2-clicks-to-span, **trust calibration BY CLAIM TYPE** (supported→up; partial/unsupported/conflicting→down/calibrated — never reward overtrust), SUS, NASA-TLX, SEQ, source-set selection accuracy.

 ### 9. DESIGN AUDIT (Phase-2 UI tasks) — MANDATORY for any web/UI issue (I-p2-*)
 Added 2026-05-21 (I-p2-002). Every Phase-2 UI task gets, IN ADDITION to the code-diff review, a separate **Codex DESIGN audit** at the top-tier bar, using the **16-dimension rubric** in `state/polaris_phase2_ui_breakdown_2026_05_21.md`. The design-audit brief MUST:
 - Use the dedicated template `.codex/DESIGN_AUDIT_BRIEF_FORMAT.md`.
-- **Attach rendered evidence in the loop** — Codex cannot audit design from a diff: a screenshot MATRIX (desktop/tablet/mobile + zoom + the key interaction states) captured via the **production standalone harness** (`node web/.next/standalone/server.js`, NOT `next dev`), plus Playwright trace + axe output where applicable.
+- **Attach rendered evidence in the loop** — Codex cannot audit design from a diff: a screenshot MATRIX (desktop/tablet/mobile + zoom + the key interaction states) captured via the **production standalone harness** (`node web/.next/standalone/server.js`, NOT `next dev`), plus REQUIRED Playwright trace + axe output + keyboard + screen-reader + evidence-click tests (per the dedicated template).
 - Audit all 16 dimensions (visual, user-flow, data-flow, focus, clarity, frontier-head-to-head, accessibility/WCAG-2.2-AA, provability, responsive/zoom, EN-FR-i18n-ready [EN-first waiver logged], content/microcopy, security-verified, performance, dense-table UX, role governance, independent-rendered-verification) → PASS/NEEDS-WORK each.
 - Verdict: APPROVE iff all 16 PASS. Artifacts: `outputs/audits/<id>/codex_design_audit.txt` + the screenshot matrix + Playwright traces + axe output.
 - §8.3.1 cap applies; per the v3 standard verdict rule, iter-5 residual P0/P1 → force-APPROVE + follow-up issue (operator-confirmed 2026-05-21).

 ### 9. DESIGN AUDIT (Phase-2 UI tasks) — MANDATORY for any web/UI issue (I-p2-*)
 Added 2026-05-21 (I-p2-002). Every Phase-2 UI task gets, IN ADDITION to the code-diff review, a separate **Codex DESIGN audit** at the top-tier bar, using the **16-dimension rubric** in `state/polaris_phase2_ui_breakdown_2026_05_21.md`. The design-audit brief MUST:
 - Use the dedicated template `.codex/DESIGN_AUDIT_BRIEF_FORMAT.md`.
-- **Attach rendered evidence in the loop** — Codex cannot audit design from a diff: a screenshot MATRIX (desktop/tablet/mobile + zoom + the key interaction states) captured via the **production standalone harness** (`node web/.next/standalone/server.js`, NOT `next dev`), plus Playwright trace + axe output where applicable.
+- **Attach rendered evidence in the loop** — Codex cannot audit design from a diff: a screenshot MATRIX (desktop/tablet/mobile + zoom + the key interaction states) captured via the **production standalone harness** (`node web/.next/standalone/server.js`, NOT `next dev`), plus REQUIRED Playwright trace + axe output + keyboard + screen-reader + evidence-click tests (per the dedicated template).
 - Audit all 16 dimensions (visual, user-flow, data-flow, focus, clarity, frontier-head-to-head, accessibility/WCAG-2.2-AA, provability, responsive/zoom, EN-FR-i18n-ready [EN-first waiver logged], content/microcopy, security-verified, performance, dense-table UX, role governance, independent-rendered-verification) → PASS/NEEDS-WORK each.
 - Verdict: APPROVE iff all 16 PASS. Artifacts: `outputs/audits/<id>/codex_design_audit.txt` + the screenshot matrix + Playwright traces + axe output.
 - §8.3.1 cap applies; per the v3 standard verdict rule, iter-5 residual P0/P1 → force-APPROVE + follow-up issue (operator-confirmed 2026-05-21).
